title: IDF/USAF Day 32 Strikes — 170+ Targets Hit Over Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, Parchin
detail: The IDF and USAF announced completion of a wide-scale strike wave hitting 170+ targets in the preceding 24 hours. Targets included drone engine manufacturing facilities, anti-tank missile and short-range SAM component R&D sites, warhead casting and filling factories for ballistic missiles, and military headquarters in western Iran. In Isfahan, strikes hit an underground 'missile city,' weapons depots, and military bases. Defense Secretary Hegseth stated '200 dynamic strikes alone' were conducted overnight. Trump posted video of an ammunition depot strike in Isfahan. Additional strikes hit Shiraz and Parchin military complex. Power outages were reported in Tehran after shrapnel struck a substation. US CENTCOM announced the cumulative total now exceeds 11,000 targets struck since February 28.

title: Iran Strikes Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai — Fire, Oil Spill Risk
detail: Iran struck a Kuwaiti Very Large Crude Carrier (VLCC), the MV Al Salmi, while it was anchored at the Port of Dubai at full capacity. The strike ignited a fire aboard the vessel and raised fears of a major oil spill in Dubai's port. The attack represented Iran's continued campaign targeting Gulf energy infrastructure and maritime shipping, now extending inside a major UAE commercial port. The VLCC was anchored and loaded, maximizing the potential for environmental damage. Dubai port authorities dispatched emergency response teams. The attack triggered a spike in tanker insurance premiums across the Gulf.

title: US National Gas Price Hits $4.02/Gallon — First Time Above $4 Since 2022
detail: The US national average gasoline price reached $4.02 per gallon on March 31, crossing the $4 threshold for the first time since 2022. California averaged $5.87/gallon. The milestone arrived exactly 32 days into the Iran war, reflecting the compounding impact of Hormuz blockade, Brent crude at ~$107/bbl, and refinery constraints. Trump responded to media questions by telling European and Asian allies facing fuel shortages to 'go to the Strait and just TAKE IT,' saying the US 'won't be there to help you anymore.' Energy analyst Fereidun Fesharaki warned oil could reach $150–200/barrel if Hormuz remains near-closed for another 6–8 weeks. The IEA noted that every week of near-closure represents approximately 100 million barrels of oil not flowing through the Strait.

title: Iran Formally Sends Counter-Reply to US 15-Point Peace Proposal via Pakistan
detail: Iran formally transmitted its counter-reply to the US 15-point peace proposal via Pakistan as intermediary on approximately March 31. Tehran's counter-conditions included: end to all hostilities, payment of war reparations, international guarantees against future attacks, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and cessation of attacks on Hezbollah and Iraqi militias. Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman described the US conditions as 'excessive, unrealistic and unreasonable.' Despite the formal counter, the White House press secretary said US-Iran talks were 'going well' and that Iranian officials now at the table were 'more reasonable' than before. Secretary of State Rubio confirmed objectives would be achieved in 'weeks, not months.' Pakistan's PM Sharif expressed confidence in his country's facilitation role, with China also backing the Islamabad initiative.

title: Lebanon Government Formally Bans Hezbollah Military Wing — Unprecedented Step
detail: The Lebanese government took unprecedented steps to formally classify Hezbollah's military wing as illegal, move to ban its military activities, and communicate this position to the United Nations. Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam stated that IRGC was actively directing Hezbollah operations, and condemned Iran's use of Lebanese territory as a battlefront. The Lebanese government condemned Hezbollah for attacking without state authorization and expelled the Iranian ambassador (though he remained in-country at Hezbollah's request). The steps reflect Lebanon's attempt to extricate itself from the conflict and potentially open a path to Hezbollah disarmament under a ceasefire framework — a key Israeli demand. Hezbollah continued operations regardless, having conducted 1,388 attack waves against Israel since March 2.

title: IAEA Board of Governors Convenes Emergency Session on Iran Nuclear Facility Strikes
detail: The IAEA Board of Governors convened an emergency session to assess the status of Iran's nuclear facilities following four weeks of US-Israeli strikes. The session addressed strikes on Natanz, Fordow, Arak heavy water complex, the Yazd/Ardakan yellowcake plant, and the Bushehr nuclear power plant (struck March 24). IAEA Director-General Grossi called the Bushehr strike 'the reddest line' of nuclear safety. Rosatom warned the situation was 'deteriorating,' with ~480 Russian nationals on site and evacuations being prepared. Gulf cities within 175–373 miles of Bushehr — Kuwait City, Manama, Doha, Dubai — faced potential radiological exposure risk. Iran's UN ambassador reiterated the attacks constitute 'a war crime and a crime against humanity.' Iranian hardline lawmakers continued pushing NPT withdrawal legislation.
